metadata.title: Dense Analysis Wide Table With Callouts

slides.title: Control Maturity by Domain
slides.strapline: IDENTIFY | High-density chart-table-commentary stress case intended to pressure-test split behavior and whitespace management in the most constrained analytical layout.
slides.heading: Domain-level scorecard
body: Detect and Protect remain the weakest domains based on both score and trend direction, while Respond maintains the strongest relative operating maturity.
Govern and Identify show moderate scores but exhibit uneven quarter-over-quarter movement, indicating inconsistent remediation execution quality.
Cross-domain spread remains wide enough to warrant differentiated intervention plans instead of a single enterprise-wide uplift playbook.
Prioritize dependency-heavy controls where low maturity overlaps with high business criticality and repeat finding recurrence.
Strengthen second-line challenge routines for domains with persistent evidence-quality gaps and delayed closure confirmation.
Track outcomes with standardized maturity definitions to prevent score inflation and improve comparability across reporting periods.
Tie remediation funding and staffing decisions to expected score uplift and closure velocity improvements by domain.
Publish domain-level accountability heatmaps to reinforce ownership and accelerate management intervention on lagging streams.

callouts.heading: Risk Concentration
body: Weakest domains cluster around preventive and detective controls.
Recurrence risk remains highest in low-scoring domains.

callouts.heading: Recommended Focus
body: Standardize evidence quality and closure definitions.
Escalate aging outliers at monthly steering forums.

chart.source: Source: domain maturity assessment extracts (illustrative stress data).

slides.noteSource: Source: domain scorecard and management tracker extracts (illustrative stress data).
